We visited the Japanese Tea Garden on May 31, 2026.  We had a free afternoon and decided to venture out from the downtown area.  We took public transportation to the Japanese Tea Garden.  The garden is in Golden Gate Park, a huge and beautiful green space just outside of the downtown area. The garden itself is small and easy to walk around.  It was incredibly calming and enjoyable.  We purchased Japanese ice cream mochi at the cafe and enjoyed watching the squirrels attempt to steal food from the visitors' plates.  Visiting here was an afterthought, but I would go back again and again if I lived in the area.  If we had more time, I would love to have explored the entire Golden Gate Park.  After the Japanese Tea Garden, we jumped back on public transportation and went all the way to the beach, then back to town.

It was really seems be in Japan! Need to be visit! Totally in love with this place.
Inside you can taste something at the bar, I grabbed a good the. And you have also a little nice shop.

Upon entering the Garden, you may wish to stop at the Tea House for hot green tea and Tea House Cookies.  It overlooks a pond with beautiful greenery.  The gardens are beautiful and easy to walk through.  Koi fish are in the ponds.  Quiet and thoughtful experience.  It takes about an hour to enjoy the Tea House and Gardens.  The gift shop is filled with Japanese items.  Highly recommend visiting the Conservatory and Botanical Gardens with a Garden Pass.  You can purchase the pass online.
